schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: The device that owns the HARBOR through which workflows voyage. It keeps the AGGREGATE REGISTER of the fleet (the TRUTH rung — what is in port, what is still at sea, where each boat stands) and grants CLEARANCE for a workflow's state-transition (the AUTHORITY rung — owner-gated, delegable through its gate). It does NOT own the boats (each workflow is owned by its intention), does NOT execute (the crew sails under delegated clearance — the harbor never sails), and does NOT own the rules (transition legality is base-class physics). 'Where you go to query open tickets.' Built in rungs: child a (this file's register.py) is the register alone — a compiled index over two vantages already on disk (OPEN boats = tickets voyaging in CairnCommons/tickets/; IN-PORT boats = components with a history.json beside their code). Primitives (cairn/harbor_master/register.py): `register()` -> {open, in_port, fleet, counts}; `open_boats(reg)` / `in_port(reg)` / `find(reg, id)`.
why: A workflow-transition factors into THREE rungs (MAP.md, the emit-chokepoint trichotomy resolved 2026-07-21): RULES (is this transition legal for this class? — base-class physics, inherited, un-delegable), AUTHORITY (who may invoke it? — owner-gated, delegable per-operation, non-ambient, Law 6), and TRUTH (record it — local in each boat's own history, central in this register). This gives the record-and-clearance half of the chokepoint a HOME without becoming the ground_loop-executor goof: decision local, record central, execution never here. The register is compiled inference at FLEET scale (Law 1) — 'what is open' answered from structure, not re-derived by walking every boat by hand — the same move as the per-ticket projector (charter-state-history-split), one scale out. It is an INDEX, never a rival record (Law 7): every entry's standing is read from the boat's own cursor and points back to it; the register invents no truth a boat does not already hold, so it cannot drift from the fleet it describes.
invoke: import: `from cairn.harbor_master import register`. Query the live fleet: `python3 -m cairn.harbor_master.register`. Prove: `python3 cairn/harbor_master/proofs/test_harbor_register.py` (exit 0 = green), or under the tester via `TesterDevice().run_proof(...)`. Dependency-light — pure file reads + the projector's pure core; no db_domain, no network; runs bare.
node_class: code-seam (device by role — the harbor). Today it is the register primitive (child a); the CLEARANCE gate (child b, waits on the base-class emit-chokepoint), the JOURNEY view (child c, deferred to meet the web_server pane), and the BaseDevice introspection FACE + bus-fronting are later rungs — filed edges below, each deferred until the thing that pulls it exists (as inference_domain and db_domain deferred their faces).
entry: FRESH design, ordinary mechanism. The design — the harbor that gates movement and keeps the register but never sails, the three-rung factoring of a transition (rules/authority/truth), the register as compiled inference one scale out from the per-ticket projector — is Cairn's, converged with Akien 2026-07-21 (the /sorted that landed the harbor-master metaphor and resolved the emit-chokepoint into a trichotomy). The mechanism is pure file reads + the projector's pure core; nothing crossed as carried code. The register is computed on read, so child a's filed db-vs-git placement edge DISSOLVES — there is no stored second copy to place.
state_and_history: compiled to state.json (projected window) + history.json (append-only voyage) beside this charter — the charter-state-history split; this file stays pure intention+why, the operational voyage lives in the sidecars (Law 1/4/7)
filed_edges: (a) THE CLEARANCE GATE (child b) — grants/refuses a transition through the owner's gate (authority rung), delegable to an igor per-operation, non-ambient (Law 6); records the movement. Waits on the base-class emit-chokepoint / transition-validation physics (state-machine-physics.json — the rules rung it clears against). The method-registry (Law 8, retired with the driver-executor cairn 584aa74) RETURNS here — re-instated, not re-derived.

(b) THE JOURNEY VIEW (child c) — follow one boat across its whole life: an open ticket and its berthed history are two vantages of one voyage (`find()` already returns BOTH by id today, un-stitched). Stitching them by a journey_id is deferred to meet the web_server's journey pane (web-server child d).

(c) DONE-BUT-NOT-BERTHED is surfaced, not resolved: a ticket at cursor PROVED still living in tickets/ shows as an OPEN boat standing PROVED — migration debt the harbor makes visible (Law 7, honest index). Auto-berthing a PROVED ticket into its component history is a later move, not child a's.

(d) COMPUTED ON READ — no stored register. If fleet queries turn hot, an event-fired cache (the FileChanged pattern the intentions_model_compiler uses) is the grow-against-need step; never a poll. Do not add it before a hot query exists (build-minimal).

(e) ID NORMALIZATION — a boat's ticket id (hyphen: `harbor-master`) and its berthed dir (underscore: `harbor_master`) differ; `find()` matches literally today. Normalizing so one boat's two vantages join under one id is child c's concern (the journey stitch).
gates: proof gate (tester) — a proof a hollow build could not pass: QUERY the fleet and get BOTH open and in-port boats non-empty with their standings (a register that can only show one berth proves nothing about querying the fleet — the non-hollow floor); show the register is an INDEX over the boats' own truths, not a rival record (every in-port standing is byte-equal to project(that boat's history).cursor; every open standing is read from the ticket's own state — drift reds it, Law 7); show every boat points back to a source that exists (the index references, never replaces); show open ∪ in_port is exhaustive and berth-disjoint (no boat invented or lost). Durability gate: proven, persisted beside the proof via validation_store.persist_validation, committed.
falsifier: RED on any of: (1) the register shows only one berth over the real fleet (open OR in_port empty) — it cannot answer 'query the fleet', the whole point; (2) a register entry's standing diverges from the boat's own record (an invented or stale truth — Law 7 breach, a rival record not an index); (3) a boat's source does not exist on disk (the index points at nothing); (4) a boat appears in neither or is silently dropped (fleet != open ∪ in_port). Wrong-intent signals (the design slips Akien named): if the harbor starts EXECUTING or SCHEDULING work it is the ground_loop-executor goof; if it comes to OWN the boats (mutating a workflow's content, not just indexing its movement) it has become the fleet owner, not the harbor; if devices advance workflows AROUND it once the clearance gate lands (ambient transitions), the authority rung has failed (Law 6).
how_it_learns: Not yet, and honestly so — child a is a pure index; it holds no gate whose evidence it could accumulate. Its learning surface OPENS with child b (the clearance gate): once transitions pass through the harbor, the register becomes the evidence of how the fleet actually moves — which transitions stall, where boats pile up, which clearances get delegated — the raw material for learning the gates (the learns-its-gates founding intention). Today it only COMPILES the fleet's standings; it does not yet judge them. The register being an honest index now is the precondition for that learning later — you cannot learn from a record that drifts.
traces_to: Law 1 (the aggregate is compiled, not re-derived — fleet-scale compiled inference, the projector one scale out)
Law 7 (the register is a record of truth: an index over the boats' own histories, append-only in each boat, never a rival that drifts)
Law 6 (exactly one owner; the clearance rung is owner-gated, delegated only through the gate — child b)
Law 4 (the rules are base-class physics, not the harbor's to grant — gates, not supervisors)
Law 8 (the method a workflow-string resolves to enters only proven — the method-registry returns with the clearance gate)
build order: harbor_master, after the heartbeat+bus+system-device+inference+web-server block; waits on the charter-split (done) + the base-class emit-chokepoint (child b's dependency) — MAP.md:996
owner: CC owns harbor_master's code + proofs and gates writes to them. Akien owns the design ratification (the harbor-master metaphor and the three-rung transition factoring, converged 2026-07-21: 'looks good to me') and the node-class definitions. Each boat is owned by its own intention — the harbor indexes the fleet, it does not own the boats.
proof: proofs/test_harbor_register.py — the fleet queries with both berths non-empty (non-hollow); the register is an index not a rival record (in-port standings byte-equal to project(history).cursor, open standings read from the ticket's own state); every boat points back to an existing source; open ∪ in_port is exhaustive and disjoint; the reflexive tooth — the harbor sees its OWN berthed history (in port) AND its own open ticket (open), the two-vantage truth and proof the scan reaches both roots. Run by the tester, sealed beside the proof. Green: `python3 cairn/harbor_master/proofs/test_harbor_register.py`.
provenance: Born 2026-07-24 as the next step of the code spine, on the foundation the charter-state-history split made uniform (child c PROVED 2026-07-24 — every component's history is now the same shape, which is exactly what makes a fleet-scale index over them possible). Cast 2026-07-21 via /sorted as the harbor-master ticket; child a (the register) is the first buildable rung because its sole dependency (the split, whose local histories it aggregates) has landed — child b waits on the base-class emit-chokepoint, child c is deferred. Built minimal (the register alone), grown against need. Does not yet retire any CLAUDE.md IOU; advances Telos 4/5 (the self-hosting record of workflow) from thesis toward a queryable fleet.
